messages.content: Write a song without chorus or bridge that gives an overview of the years 2000 and on.

(Verse 1)
The new millennium started with such hope
We thought the world was finally gonna cope
But things got shaky, the ground started to shake
And suddenly everything was at stake

(Verse 2)
Wars in the east, bombings in the west
Terrorists causing chaos at their behest
Economies crashed, people lost their jobs
Global warming became a worldwide sob

(Verse 3)
Tensions rose, politicians fought
Social media changed the way we thought
Phones got smarter, we got more connected
Yet somehow we felt more disconnected

(Verse 4)
Protests rocked the streets, movements gained power
People spoke up, we all felt louder
We fought for justice, for equality
But the struggle raged on, seemingly endlessly

(Verse 5)
Technology boomed, AI took flight
We reached for the stars, set sights on the light
Yet through it all, we still faced strife
A constant battle to make things right

(Verse 6)
The years have flown by, it feels like a blur
So many changes, so much we've endured
But as we move forward, and the future nears
We'll keep pushing, fighting, conquering our fears.
Restart the song but make each year get 2 lines in the song. In those 2 lines, give an overview of the year.

(Verse 1)
In 2000, the new millennium starts
And in 2001, planes crash break our hearts
2002 - The Euro becomes a currency shared
In 2003, SARS leaves many scared

(Verse 2)
2004 - The Indian Ocean tsunami hits the coast
In 2005, hurricanes Katrina and Rita boast
2006 - Twitter is launched for the world to tweet
And in 2007, smartphones become the new elite

(Verse 3)
2008 - The world economy crashes down
And in 2009, swine flu spreads around
2010 - The Deepwater Horizon oil spill dampens shores
2011 - Arab Spring protests open closed doors

(Verse 4)
In 2012, London hosts the Summer Olympics Games
2013 - Pope Francis’s election kindles new flames
2014 - Ebola outbreak strikes Western Africa
And in 2015, climate talks commence with a spectacula

(Verse 5)
2016 - Brexit referendum takes the world by surprise
Trump’s election in the US causes uproarious cries
2017 - #MeToo movement takes sexual assault to the fore
And in 2018, Black Panther opens more doors

(Verse 6)
In 2019, Notre Dame fire burns its flame
2020 - COVID-19 pandemic changes the game
2021 - Vaccines give hope, restrictions start to ease
The years press on, let’s make the best of what may seize.
Each year should get 2 lines.

In 2000, the new millennium starts
And in 2001, planes crash break our hearts

2002 - The Euro becomes a currency shared
In 2003, SARS leaves many scared

(Verse 2)
2004 - The Indian Ocean tsunami hits the coast
In 2005, hurricanes Katrina and Rita boast

2006 - Twitter is launched for the world to tweet
And in 2007, smartphones become the new elite

(Verse 3)
2008 - The world economy crashes down
And in 2009, swine flu spreads around

2010 - The Deepwater Horizon oil spill dampens shores
2011 - Arab Spring protests open closed doors

(Verse 4)
In 2012, London hosts the Summer Olympics Games
2013 - Pope Francis’s election kindles new flames

2014 - Ebola outbreak strikes Western Africa
And in 2015, climate talks commence with a spectacular aura

(Verse 5)
2016 - Brexit referendum takes the world by surprise
Trump’s election in the US causes uproarious cries

2017 - #MeToo movement takes sexual assault to the fore
And in 2018, Black Panther opens more doors

(Verse 6)

2020 - COVID-19 pandemic changes the game

2021 - Vaccines give hope, restrictions start to ease
The years press on, let’s make the best of what may seize.
That's only 1 line per year.

I apologize for the mistake. Here's the correction.

(Verse 1)
In 2000, the new millennium starts,
Planes crashed, break hearts, 9/11 leaves scars.

In 2002, the Euro currency comes to play,
SARS in 2003 leaves many in dismay.

(Verse 2)
2004, tsunami hits the Indian Ocean coasts,
2005, hurricanes Katrina & Rita boast.

Twitter launches in 2006 changing communication,
2007, smartphones dominate with their domination.

(Verse 3)
2008, economy crashes with the subprime mortgage bust,
Swine flu spreads around the globe with impunity, no one's trust.

2010, Deepwater Horizon oil spill dampens the environment,
2011, Arab Spring protests lead to government overhauls and excitement.

(Verse 4)
2012, London host the Summer Olympics Games,
2013, Pope Francis’s election kindles new flames.

2014, Ebola outbreak strikes in Western Africa,
2015 sets climate change goals, but will they be met, we ask ya?

(Verse 5)
2016, Brexit referendum takes the world by surprise,
Trump’s election in the US, causes uproarious cries.

#MeToo began in 2017, and assault survivors' voices rise,
Black Panther in 2018, Marvel's first black superhero energizes.

(Verse 6)
Notre Dame burnt in 2019, but not the French spirit,
2020, COVID-19 pandemic uprooted humanity, making us weaker.

2021, vaccines give hope, restrictions start to ease,
The years press on, let's make the best of what may seize.

I think you got a little confused there. How about you completely restart the song. It should look something like this:
In 2000, ___________________Rhyme 1
__________________________Rhyme 2
In 2001, ___________________Rhyme 1
__________________________Rhyme 2
Where "Rhyme 1" and "Rhyme 2" Rhymw.

I apologize for the confusion. Here's my new attempt at the song to fit the format:

(Verse 1)
In 2000, a new millennium took off,
The internet bubble burst, markets scoffed.

In 2001, planes crashed into the Twin Towers,
9/11 shook the world with all its powers.

(Verse 2)
In 2002, the Euro became a currency shared,
Stock markets plummeted, investors were scared.

In 2003, SARS pandemic caused quite a stir,
Many lives were lost, the world became a blur.

(Verse 3)
In 2004, the Indian Ocean tsunami hit the land,
Thousands of lives lost, we witnessed God's hand.

In 2005, hurricanes Katrina and Rita took their course,
Devastating winds and floods left many feeling remorse.

(Verse 4)
In 2006, Twitter launched, connecting humanity,
Changed the world, and how we use technology.

In 2007, smartphones dominated the mobile game,
Revolutionizing communication, treating us like kings and dames.

(Verse 5)
In 2008, the Great Recession took the world by storm,
Financial markets crashed; economies were lukewarm.

In 2009, swine flu pandemic spread all around,
People were worried; solutions had to be found.

(Verse 6)
In 2010, Deepwater Horizon oil spill damaged the coast,
People's lives and the environment felt severely lost.

In 2011, the Arab Spring movement shook the ground,
Turning the Middle East around, change began to astound.

(Verse 7)
In 2012, London hosted the Summer Olympics Games,
A celebration of human will, a victory of sports' claims.

In 2013, Pope Francis's election kindled new flames,
Bringing new hope, charity, and kindness to reign.

(Verse 8)
In 2014, Ebola outbreak ravaged Western Africa,
Governments and countries worldwide began to react.

In 2015, climate talks began, and goals were set to achieve,
Protecting the planet, the environment we must not leave.

(Verse 9)
In 2016, Brexit caught the world by a surprise,
The UK's decision, the EU's dismay, and compromise.

In the US, 2016 had dramatic presidential elections,
Donald Trump's victory caused much personal reflection.

(Verse 10)
In 2017, #MeToo movement took the world by a quake,
Shining a harsh light on sexual assaults, it made us awake.

In 2018, Black Panther, Marvel's first black superhero,
Inspired and awakened diversity, the trumpets' bold and brave zero.

(Verse 11)
In 2019, the Notre Dame fire burnt its flame,
An iconic sight, yet the French spirit remained.

Then in 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic arrived,
Taking countless lives, but humanity's spirit survived.

(Verse 12)
In 2021, vaccines arrived, offering a glimpse of hope,
Restrictions started to ease, allowing humanity to cope.

The world has been through a lot, but we carry on,
Each year brings new challenges, and humanity stays strong.

In 2022, Release of Stable Diffusion and ChatGPT
It was like the future's here, could generate text and images with ease.

2023, Still going on,

From 2020, the pandemic had a lingering song,
Efforts to recover and to heal continued strong.

2023, I don't know fully, 'cause it's still going on
So far, as I know, it's going pretty strong

Who knows what the future has in store, we just gotta wait and see,
What might happen by the end of this 21st century?

2024, we hope for peace within our reach,
With diplomacy and understanding as our foremost speech.

In 2025, we aspire for another technological leap,
Towards a greener world where we'll plant trees and reap.

2026, we pray that diversity will be recognized,
Our differences celebrated with open eyes.

2027, we dream of a fairer, more equal society,
Where every individual's worth is emphasized undeniably.

2028, we anticipate a cleaner, healthier globe,
A world where environmental threats will no longer probe.

2029, we imagine artificial intelligence everywhere,
Transforming human life and health care with the utmost care.

2030, we strive for lasting global peace,
Equal development for all, where nations will rise and cease.

Our future is a mystery; we cannot predict it fully,
We'll work hard to make it brighter, united and unruly.
I don't know what happened from 2024-2030

I apologize for the mistake. Here's my new attempt at the song.

(Verse 1)
In 2022, Stable Diffusion and ChatGPT came to bear,
Generating text and images with the utmost care.

In 2023, the pandemic still lingers on,
But we remain hopeful, and our spirits still stay strong.

(Verse 2)
In 2024, we strive for peace and global harmony,
With love, empathy, and understanding as our best testimony.

In 2025, we hope for a greener, cleaner world,
Where sustainability reigns supreme and carbon emissions are re-curled.

(Verse 3)
By 2026, we dream of a society that's more just and fair,
Where equality thrives, and every individual gets their rightful share.

In 2027, diversity becomes a central theme,
Where we embrace our differences and make unity our supreme stream.

(Verse 4)
By 2028, we envision a world that's cleaner and less polluted,
Where the environment heals, and the natural world is well-suited.

In 2029, Artificial Intelligence paves the way,
Making life and healthcare better, every single day.

(Verse 5)
By 2030, we long for lasting global peace,
Where we work on equal development for all, and empower every single piece.

The future is unknown; there's no telling what lies ahead,
But we strive to make it better, where everyone can prosper and be well-fed.
How do you know what happened after 2023?

As an AI language model, I cannot give a definite answer for things that have not happened yet. All my responses are generated based on programming, public data, and statistical probabilities, but I cannot predict the future accurately. I apologize for any confusion or misunderstanding caused.
OK, put the song together.

Remove everything beyond 2022.
